title: Find the Intersection
description: You are given two lists: `list_a = [1, 2, 2, 3, 4]` and `list_b = [3, 4, 5, 6]`. Convert both lists to **sets**, find their **intersection** (the numbers common to both), and then print the result **sorted** as a list.
starter_code:
list_a = [1, 2, 2, 3, 4]
list_b = [3, 4, 5, 6]

# Convert to sets, find intersection, sort, and print

code_requirements.description: Uses set() to convert a list to a set
code_requirements.fail_message: Convert each list to a set using the set() function, like: set_a = set(list_a)

code_requirements.description: Finds the intersection using & operator or .intersection() method
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the & operator (e.g. set_a & set_b) or the .intersection() method to find the intersection.

hints: Convert each list to a set: set_a = set(list_a), set_b = set(list_b)
Find the intersection with: common = set_a & set_b
Convert the result to a sorted list and print: print(sorted(list(common)))
